title: Job Description Decomposition and Evidence Mapping · Career Claim Defense
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for job description decomposition and evidence mapping and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for role requirement and evidence matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording.
Classify stated requirements as essential evidence, likely preference, negotiable proxy, process constraint, or unresolved question.
Map each priority outcome to direct, adjacent, transferable, missing, or contradictory candidate evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Outcome Extraction · changed case · Quiz Outcome Extraction
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. The quiz variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording.

For Outcome Extraction · changed case · Quiz Outcome Extraction, keep Outcome Extraction connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A listing mixes essential outcomes, preferences, recruiter keywords, copied requirements, legal text, and unknown team constraints behind a familiar title. steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Outcome Extraction
steps.instruction:
Order the Outcome Extraction evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Outcome Extraction.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Outcome Extraction assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Go No Go

content.title: Evidence defense
